title: anthonotha lamprophylla
intro: Anthonotha lamprophylla, also known as the 'Kola nut tree', is a species of tree that belongs to the family Lamiaceae. The plant is traditionally used in West African countries for its medicinal properties, which include treating fever, headaches, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Anthonotha lamprophylla are attributed to its analgesic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, making it useful for relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. In herbal preparations, the leaves and seeds of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions that can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
